title: box.py teardown leaks the DigitalOcean volume (no detach/delete on down)
description: cmd_down/_destroy_box_droplet (scripts/box.py ~2920-3003) deletes the droplet but never detaches or deletes the DO volume that _ensure_box_storage (~1889) created and attached during provision. Result: (1) every teardown orphans a paid DO volume; (2) re-provisioning the same box_id can fail with 'already attached ... Detach it before reusing' (~1915) because do_find_volume_by_name finds the same-named volume still bound to the deleted droplet. The dry-run preview (_emit_box_down_dry_run ~2881) only mentions the droplet, so the gap is invisible on review. Fix: after droplet deletion, if box.volume_id is set, best-effort detach then 'doctl compute volume delete <id> --force'; surface a 'volume' step in BOTH the dry-run and real payloads; guard against deleting a volume still attached elsewhere. Found during 2026-05-23 fresh-eyes audit; intentionally NOT auto-fixed because it is a destructive change that cannot be tested against live doctl without infra.

title: Audit follow-ups 2026-05-19: dry-run contract and lifecycle safety
description:
Fresh codebase-audit handoff from 2026-05-19. This epic covers confirmed new High findings only; older 2026-05-17 audit follow-ups remain in skillbox-audit-followups-20260517-t4j.

## Repo signals
- overlay --dry-run currently writes SKILLBOX_OVERLAY_STATE.
- runtime MCP mutating tools dispatch sync/up/down/restart/bootstrap/onboard without enforcing dry_run=true despite tool contracts.
- legacy runtime up/restart return EXIT_OK when start_services reports failed/timeout/blocked.
- install.sh --force can rm -rf the resolved --repo-dir without proving it is a Skillbox checkout or a safe target.
- scripts/07-build-and-push-binary.sh interpolates user/env values into shell strings before privileged remote Docker symlink work.

## Integrity gap
The core agent-safety contract says dry-run first and machine-readable failure status, but multiple entry points currently rely on caller discipline or return success on failure.

## Skill combo
codebase-audit -> smart -> beads-br -> divide-and-conquer; each child should start from a crisp describe/reproduce test and close with focused tests plus git diff --check.

## Execution plan
Split by write boundary: overlay/sbp, MCP dispatcher, runtime CLI lifecycle, installer safety, and binary-push script. Do not mix these with the older fleet-performance lane.

## Closeout evidence
- Child-specific focused tests pass.
- python3 -m unittest discover -s tests is rerun, or existing disk-pressure blocker is cited with exact command output.
- make doctor and make dev-sanity are rerun, or the same blocker is cited.
- br sync --flush-only exports the graph.

comments.author: b
comments.text: Addendum: child `.6` tracks the worker broker finding from the runtime audit: `worker-submit` uses blocking `subprocess.run` with a 300s launch timeout, so brokered worker runs are not truly asynchronous/durable yet. Treat it as part of the same runtime safety/reliability remediation wave, with write scope `.env-manager/runtime_manager/shared.py`, CLI worker handlers, and `tests/test_worker_runtime.py`.

title: Make overlay dry-run non-mutating end to end
description:
## Finding
`python3 .env-manager/manage.py overlay on <name> --dry-run --format json` writes persistent overlay state anyway, and `scripts/sbp overlay ... --dry-run` follows by running `skill sync` without `--dry-run`.

## Affected paths
- .env-manager/runtime_manager/cli.py:2906-3032
- .env-manager/runtime_manager/skill_visibility.py:466-483,521-562
- scripts/sbp:876-880
- tests/test_skill_visibility.py
- tests/test_cli_wrappers.py

## Root cause
`_handle_overlay` calls `_apply_persistent_overlay_action` and `_overlay_removed_links` before considering `args.dry_run`; the wrapper does not propagate dry-run to the follow-up policy sync.

## Describe / reproduce contract
Given `SKILLBOX_OVERLAY_STATE` points at an empty temp path, when `overlay on audit-dryrun --dry-run --format json` runs, then the state file must not be created and JSON must describe planned state without claiming persistence. Given `sbp overlay marketing --dry-run`, when it reaches policy sync, then any follow-up `skill sync` must be skipped or run with `--dry-run`.

## Validation commands
- `python3 -m unittest tests.test_skill_visibility tests.test_cli_wrappers`
- `tmp=$(mktemp -d); SKILLBOX_OVERLAY_STATE="$tmp/overlays" python3 .env-manager/manage.py overlay on audit-dryrun --dry-run --format json; test ! -e "$tmp/overlays"`
- `git diff --check`

## Closeout evidence
Show the focused tests and temp-state probe passing. Do not touch unrelated overlay policy.

title: Enforce runtime MCP dry-run-first for mutating tools
description:
## Finding
The runtime MCP server advertises dry-run-first for mutating tools, but `dispatch_tool` calls `manage.py` directly when `dry_run` is omitted. Confirmed for `skillbox_sync`, `skillbox_up`, `skillbox_down`, `skillbox_restart`, `skillbox_bootstrap`, and `skillbox_onboard`.

## Affected paths
- .env-manager/mcp_server.py:682-832,1416-1479,1487-1521,1696-1720
- tests/test_mcp_server.py

## Root cause
Tool metadata and initialize instructions encode the safety rule as prose, not as dispatcher policy. `_dispatch_manage_tool` validates identifiers and required positionals, then always calls `run_manage`.

## Describe / pass-fail contract
Given a mutating MCP tool with `dry_run_required` semantics, when the tool is called without `dry_run=true`, then the MCP response must be `isError=true` with a structured `dry_run_required` error and `run_manage` must not be called. Given the same tool with `dry_run=true`, when it succeeds, then the preview response should include enough subject/marker context to allow the corresponding real action if that command is intentionally allowed.

## Validation commands
- `python3 -m unittest tests.test_mcp_server`
- No-side-effect monkeypatch proof: dispatch each mutating tool without dry_run and assert `run_manage` was not invoked.
- `git diff --check`

## Closeout evidence
Show tests for missing dry-run, allowed dry-run, and at least one scoped real-action policy if implemented. Keep read-only tools unaffected.

title: Return nonzero when legacy up or restart service starts fail
description:
## Finding
Legacy `up` and `restart` paths return `EXIT_OK` even when `start_services(...)` returns entries with `result: failed`, `timeout`, or `blocked`. Local-runtime profiles already classify these failures in `workflows._up_services_payload`.

## Affected paths
- .env-manager/runtime_manager/cli.py:3195-3244,3269-3312
- .env-manager/runtime_manager/workflows.py:3912-3980
- tests/test_runtime_manager.py or focused CLI unit tests

## Root cause
The legacy path packages raw `service_results` and hardcodes `EXIT_OK` in `_handle_up` and `_handle_restart`; it never maps unhealthy service result values into an error envelope or nonzero exit.

## Describe / reproduce contract
Given `start_services` returns `[{"id":"svc","result":"failed"}]`, when legacy `_handle_up(... --format json)` runs, then it must return `EXIT_ERROR` and emit a structured error naming the failed service. Given all service starts are `started` or `already-running`, the existing success output and exit 0 must remain unchanged. Apply the same rule to restart start results.

## Validation commands
- `python3 -m unittest tests.test_runtime_manager tests.test_text_renderers`
- Add a no-side-effect unit test that monkeypatches `start_services` to return `failed` and asserts nonzero.
- `git diff --check`

## Closeout evidence
Show failing-service and success-regression tests. Keep local-profile `LOCAL_RUNTIME_START_BLOCKED` behavior aligned rather than duplicating a divergent error shape.

title: Guard install.sh --force against dangerous target deletion
description:
## Finding
`install.sh --force` runs `rm -rf` on the resolved `--repo-dir` if it exists and is non-empty, with no denylist for `/`, `$HOME`, repo parents, or non-Skillbox directories.

## Affected paths
- install.sh:361-367,620-664,1182-1230
- tests/test_install_script.py

## Root cause
`ensure_checkout_target_ready` treats every user-supplied target as disposable once `--force` is set. It does not require a Skillbox sentinel, a safe install-root shape, or a backup/rename path before deletion.

## Describe / pass-fail contract
Given `--repo-dir /`, `$HOME`, the current repo parent, or a non-Skillbox temp directory containing a sentinel file, when install runs with `--force`, then it must exit nonzero and leave the sentinel untouched. Given an existing Skillbox checkout target with `.env-manager/manage.py` and `--force`, then replacement remains possible through a safer backup/rename flow or an explicit approved path.

## Validation commands
- `python3 -m unittest tests.test_install_script`
- `bash -n install.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Closeout evidence
Show destructive-target regression tests that prove user data is preserved. Avoid broad installer rewrites outside target safety.

title: Harden binary push script shell and remote-command quoting
description:
## Finding
`scripts/07-build-and-push-binary.sh` interpolates `SKILLBOX_BUILD_APT_DEPS`, `PKG`, `BIN_NAME`, `TARGET_BIN_DIR`, and `SYMLINK_DIR` into local `bash -c` and remote SSH shell strings, then uses privileged Docker on the target to write symlinks.

## Affected paths
- scripts/07-build-and-push-binary.sh:40-63,75-88,93
- tests/test_operational_scripts.py or a focused script test

## Root cause
The helper accepts free-form identifiers/paths/dependency strings and builds shell snippets rather than validating safe tokens and quoting every remote command component. The blast radius is higher because the target-side symlink step runs privileged Docker with `/` mounted.

## Describe / pass-fail contract
Given a bin/package name or target path containing shell metacharacters, when the script is invoked, then it must reject the input before any Docker or SSH command runs. Given valid identifiers and paths, then generated docker/ssh/scp command arguments must preserve behavior and pass `bash -n`.

## Validation commands
- `bash -n scripts/07-build-and-push-binary.sh`
- Add focused tests that parse or dry-run command construction with malicious names such as `bad;touch /tmp/pwn` and assert rejection before subprocess execution.
- `git diff --check`

## Closeout evidence
Show malicious-input rejection tests and a valid-command regression. Keep the script operator-focused; do not widen it into a general deploy tool.

title: Make worker-submit return durable queued/running state instead of blocking on Hermes
description:
## Finding
`worker-submit` is documented as a brokered worker surface, but `create_worker_run` calls Hermes with blocking `subprocess.run(...)` inside submit. The default launch timeout is 300s, so a long or hung worker can tie up the CLI/MCP request and be classified as launch failure rather than a durable running job.

## Affected paths
- .env-manager/runtime_manager/shared.py:1238-1312,1315-1441
- .env-manager/runtime_manager/cli.py worker-submit/status handlers
- tests/test_worker_runtime.py

## Root cause
The submit path persists `worker.launching`, then synchronously waits for Hermes to finish and immediately loads terminal result files. There is no PID/heartbeat-running state for `worker-status` to reconcile.

## Describe / pass-fail contract
Given Hermes starts successfully but does not finish immediately, when `worker-submit` is called, then it should return quickly with a durable `queued` or `running` state and a run id. Given the worker later writes `result.json`, when `worker-status <run_id>` runs, then it should reconcile to the terminal result and expose artifacts. Given Hermes cannot start, launch failure should remain structured and immediate.

## Validation commands
- `python3 -m unittest tests.test_worker_runtime`
- Add a fake long-running Hermes test that proves submit returns before timeout and status can later reconcile.
- `git diff --check`

## Closeout evidence
Show async submit/status tests and preserve existing immediate-failure behavior. Keep learning-promotion review gates unchanged.

title: Audit follow-ups 2026-05-17: MCP ssh hardening regression + perf/bug cleanup
description:
Bundles the 7 open issues surfaced by /codebase-audit on 2026-05-17 after the sec-hardening-20260516 slice closed. Two file-bounded sequential lanes — not 7-wide parallelism.

## Lane A: operator_mcp_server.py (sequential)

1. **H-SEC-1 + H-SEC-2 (P1)** — close the MCP ssh hardening regression. Bundled by smart-20260517-mcp-ssh-hardening-amd: add `--` separator at operator_mcp_server.py:531 and validate user/host before run_ssh at 757-769. One commit + one regression test. Drive first.
   - skillbox-audit-h-sec-1-mcp-ssh-argv-9tp
   - skillbox-audit-h-sec-2-mcp-validate-user-host-1zx
   - skillbox-smart-20260517-mcp-ssh-hardening-amd (decision wrapper)

2. **TTL mismatch (P2)** — unify dry-run marker TTL between operator_mcp_server.py and the PreToolUse guard hook. operator_mcp_server.py just shipped a 10m TTL in commit e014b19; the hook needs to match.
   - skillbox-audit-h-mcp-1-dryrun-ttl-mismatch-fdd

3. **compose_up isError (P3)** — compose_up MCP response should reflect any-step failure, not just top-level intent.
   - skillbox-audit-h-mcp-2-compose-up-iserror-3bz

## Lane B: scripts/box.py (sequential)

1. **Parallel fleet status (P2)** — parallelize per-box fleet health in `cmd_status`.
   - skillbox-audit-h-perf-1-parallel-fleet-status-bu8

2. **Parallel ssh candidate probing (P2)** — parallel SSH candidate probing + cache last-good target.
   - skillbox-audit-h-perf-2-ssh-candidate-probing-6ug

## Cross-lane parallelism

Lane A and Lane B touch different files and can run concurrently in a swarm. Each lane's nodes are strictly sequential (same-file writes).

## Drive order

1. H-SEC-1+2 bundle (P1, regression-closer) — single commit
2. After P1: pick A2/A3 or B1/B2 as the next ready frontier
3. Close epic when all 6 children + 1 decision are resolved

title: Unify dry-run marker TTL between MCP server and guard hook
description:
operator_mcp_server.py:60 (DRYRUN_MARKER_TTL_SECONDS=600) and guard-destructive-op.sh:283 (default 3600s) disagree on marker lifetime. Agents pass the guard but get rejected by the server with no indication which layer rejected them.

**Fix:** Both layers read SKILLBOX_DRYRUN_MARKER_TTL_SECONDS with the same default (600s). Rejection message includes observed age and configured ttl.

**Spec:** agent_ergonomics_audit/AUDIT_2026-05-17.md (H-MCP-1)
**Source:** /codebase-audit 2026-05-17

title: compose_up isError reflects top-level intent, not any-step failure
description:
operator_mcp_server.py:773-798 sets isError=true on ANY step failure. A successful build + up with a failed up-surfaces reports isError=true, leading agents to redo the whole thing.

**Fix:** isError reflects success of the headline 'up' step only. Per-step results stay in steps[]. Update the description to direct agents at steps[] for partial-failure detail.

**Spec:** agent_ergonomics_audit/AUDIT_2026-05-17.md (H-MCP-2)
**Source:** /codebase-audit 2026-05-17

title: Parallelize per-box fleet health in box status
description:
box.py:3200,3217-3276 (cmd_status -> box_health) runs serially per box; a 3-box fleet takes ~60-80s.

**Fix:** Wrap the per-box loop in ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=min(5, N)). I/O-bound, independent, output shape unchanged.

**Expected:** 3-box fleet ~80s -> ~20s; scales linearly with worker count.

**Spec:** agent_ergonomics_audit/AUDIT_2026-05-17.md (H-PERF-1)
**Source:** /codebase-audit 2026-05-17

title: Parallel SSH candidate probing + cache last-good target
description:
box.py:854-892 (wait_for_ssh, resolve_box_ssh_target) probes 3 candidates serially with fixed 1s sleeps; ~45s worst case per box.

**Fix:** (a) Probe candidates in parallel, return first-success. (b) Persist winning target in inventory; try cached first on next call.

**Expected:** Cold 45s -> ~5s. Warm ~0s.

**Spec:** agent_ergonomics_audit/AUDIT_2026-05-17.md (H-PERF-2)
**Source:** /codebase-audit 2026-05-17

title: Harden ssh argv on MCP path (add -- separator)
description:
operator_mcp_server.py:531 builds ssh argv without the `--` separator that box.py uses, allowing host strings starting with `-` to be parsed as ssh flags.

**Fix:** `cmd = ["ssh", *ssh_opts, "--", f"{user}@{host}", command]` — mirror box.py:ssh_cmd().

**Spec:** agent_ergonomics_audit/AUDIT_2026-05-17.md (H-SEC-1)
**Source:** /codebase-audit 2026-05-17
close_reason: Fixed in 98a1593: added `--` separator to ssh argv in operator_mcp_server.py:run_ssh. Regression test test_run_ssh_argv_includes_double_dash_separator covers it.

title: Validate user/host in MCP run_ssh before invoking ssh
description:
operator_mcp_server.py:757-769 reads user/host from inventory and passes them straight into run_ssh. box.py defines and uses _validate_ssh_user / _validate_host but the MCP server bypasses them.

**Fix:** Import or duplicate the validators; call them before run_ssh; return invalid_box_config on failure.

**Spec:** agent_ergonomics_audit/AUDIT_2026-05-17.md (H-SEC-2)
**Source:** /codebase-audit 2026-05-17
close_reason: Fixed in 98a1593: _validate_ssh_user/_validate_host added to operator_mcp_server.py and called in handle_operator_box_exec before run_ssh. Regression tests test_box_exec_rejects_flag_injection_host and test_box_exec_rejects_malformed_ssh_user cover it.

title: Build dev-to-prod parity compiler report
description:
Smart-goal bead for idea #3 from the 2026-05-12 idea-wizard pass: build a dev-to-prod parity compiler for Skillbox.

The idea: turn the roadmap's prod-bridge thesis into an executable `parity-report`/planner surface that compares client overlay services, env files, health checks, reverse-proxy roles, deploy-skill modes, and production-stack declarations. The output should name exact missing pieces and generate reviewable preview artifacts, so Skillbox becomes a staging-grade proving layer without turning into a hosted platform or replacing app-specific CI/deploy systems.

repo_state:
  date: 2026-05-12
  head: 0691013 fix(skills): prefer path-specific scope rules
  branch: main ahead origin/main by 6 at bead creation time
  dirty_paths_at_creation: .beads/issues.jsonl, .env-manager/runtime_manager/cli.py, .gitignore, README.md, scripts/sbp, tests/test_cli_wrappers.py
context:
  source_idea: idea-wizard ranked idea #3, Dev-to-prod parity compiler
  docs_anchor: docs/ROADMAP.md Phase 2 says Skillbox is missing the bridge to the operator's nginx/deploy layer, including reverse-proxy parity, env parity, and deploy skill integration.
  implemented_surface: runtime graph, local runtime profiles, parity ledger validation, env files, client-project/open/publish, stewardship-report, and agent-readable capabilities already exist.
  open_beads: skillbox-agm broad audit/smart-goal bead; skillbox-o7j MCP extras review bead.
integrity_gap:
  The README and roadmap describe parity concepts, but there is no single inspectable command that answers whether a client overlay is actually ready to behave like a staging/devbox tier for a real prod stack.
hardening_decision:
  hardening-first by extension: build on runtime graph, parity ledger, env files, client projection, and stewardship-report instead of inventing a new deployment subsystem.
out_of_scope:
  - Do not build a hosted distributor, hosted control plane, browser IDE, or generic deploy product.
  - Do not mutate production infrastructure.
  - Do not require live nginx, registry, cloud, or Tailscale credentials for the default proof path.
  - Do not hide app-specific deploy decisions inside Skillbox; generate declarations, reports, and safe previews.
acceptance_criteria:
/goal $domain-planner $describe $reproduce process complete

One-shot goal: Design and implement the first Skillbox dev-to-prod parity compiler slice: a read-only parity report/planner that compares declared client runtime surfaces against a production-stack contract and reports reverse-proxy, env, healthcheck, and deploy-mode drift with machine-readable JSON, concise text output, and regression proof.

Success criteria:
- Start from the existing runtime graph, parity ledger, env_files, service healthcheck, local runtime mode, client projection, and stewardship-report concepts; do not create a separate deployment platform.
- Define a minimal production-stack contract that can live in a client overlay or adjacent private config and can represent reverse-proxy routes/upstreams, env var expectations, health endpoints, deploy skill modes, and optional Docker network assumptions.
- Add a read-only CLI surface with JSON output and safe first-try guidance, tentatively `python3 .env-manager/manage.py parity-report <client> --format json`, plus an MCP/tooling path if it fits existing runtime-manager patterns.
- The report must classify `ready`, `missing`, `drift`, `deferred`, and `not_assessed` domains; each finding must include the source declaration, the expected prod contract, and the next safe command or file to edit.
- Include at least one fixture client that proves the happy path and at least one fixture that proves drift across proxy, env, healthcheck, and deploy-mode domains.
- Integrate the summary into `stewardship-report` or explicitly document why the first slice keeps it separate.
- Update README/docs/ROADMAP so implemented behavior, limitations, and non-goals are separated from future roadmap prose.
- Verification must include focused unit tests for the parity planner/report, CLI JSON/text behavior, fixture drift cases, `git diff --check`, `make doctor`, and `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` unless a concrete environment blocker is recorded.
- Detached fresh-eyes review is required before completion, and this goal authorizes at least one scoped detached reviewer subagent for that gate, plus additional detached review rounds as needed: launch or route an independent reviewer that did not implement the work, have it inspect the final diff/artifacts/validation evidence, and address or explicitly block on its findings before marking the goal complete.
- Completion means validate -> commit only when authorized -> post-commit reality check; if commit authorization, credentials, or external prod fixtures block closeout, report the exact resume condition rather than calling it done.

title: Resolve critical disk pressure before broad validation
description:
Reality check: the broad unittest lane hit `No space left on device` once, and local pressure remained critical after partial temporary cleanup. Broad validation cannot be trusted while the machine may fail tests or generated artifacts from ENOSPC.

Fix direction: start with read-only pressure inventory, protect declared runtime/secret state, then either recover enough local free space for the broad suite or route broad validation through an approved offloaded path.
design:
writes:
  - none
stop_rules:
  - If free space is insufficient and no offload path is already approved, record the blocker instead of deleting data.
non_goals:
  - Do not perform Docker prune, cache deletion, or ballast changes from this node.
global_constraints:
  - Read-only by default; no deletion/truncation/moves without explicit operator approval.
  - Never touch protected agent history or secrets: ~/.codex, ~/.claude, .env, .env.box, workspace/secrets, client overlays.
  - Do not commit; integration lead owns final commit decision.
acceptance_criteria:
Done when:
* pressure-report and/or disk-space-triage inventory is recorded.
* Protected paths are not deleted, truncated, or moved.
* There is enough local free space for broad validation, or an approved offload/skip blocker is recorded.
* No cleanup is performed without explicit approval where deletion is involved.
notes: Blocked: pressure-report remains critical (13.22 GiB free, 2.87% free; df shows 13Gi available at 97% capacity). No cleanup approval and no approved offload path; broad unittest lane intentionally not run under stop rule. Result artifact: /Users/b/repos/skillbox-config/clients/skillbox/invocations/build000r/skillbox/divide-and-conquer/2026-05-19T00-38-10Z/WG-004_RESULT.md

title: Fix up/down dependency graph recovery for unhealthy already-running services
description:
Context: The operator hit `sbp up` from /Users/b/repos/cycle-chef and got spaps + htma_server reported as already-running while the API summary showed both health URLs down. This is not only a Cycle Chef issue; it is an upstream local-runtime dependency graph contract issue for any client overlay using managed services, depends_on, and healthchecks.

Problem: runtime_ops._start_service currently returns already-running as soon as a managed pid file resolves to a live pid. It does this before checking the declared healthcheck. That lets an unhealthy live process satisfy a dependency edge and makes `up` look successful while downstream services start against dead APIs. Text rendering also prints bootstrap task rows as unknown when local-runtime summaries use status instead of result.

Success contract:
- A live pid with a failing declared healthcheck is not treated as successful already-running.
- `up` either repairs the service through the normal declared command path after clearing stale/unhealthy runtime state, or returns a structured failed/blocked result that stops the graph and points to status/logs/restart.
- Healthy pre-existing HTTP/port/process/path services are still reused without spawning duplicates.
- Dependency graph startup does not continue after a dependency fails or times out.
- Text output for bootstrap tasks renders status/result clearly, never harmless unknown for planned/ready/completed rows.

Validation target: focused unittest coverage for runtime_ops/start_services, local-runtime up graph failure behavior, text rendering task rows, plus python3 -m unittest for touched test modules. Link evidence back to skillbox-config runtime normalization bead skillbox-config-sbp-up-runtime-tree-m9z.3.
close_reason: Implemented unhealthy live-pid recovery, dependency blocking, task status rendering, and focused validation; dev-sanity caveat recorded for unrelated stale skill installs.

comments.author: b
comments.text: Implemented upstream local-runtime lifecycle fix. Code changes: runtime_ops now checks declared service health before reusing a live managed pid; unhealthy live-pid services are stopped and restarted through the declared command path when possible; failed dependencies now cause downstream services to return result=blocked instead of starting; workflows maps blocked to LOCAL_RUNTIME_START_BLOCKED; text renderer uses status when bootstrap summaries do not carry result. Regression coverage added in tests/test_local_runtime.py and tests/test_text_renderers.py. Validation passed: py_compile for touched files; python3 -m unittest tests.test_local_runtime.BridgeBackedTaskTests.test_start_services_restarts_unhealthy_live_pid tests.test_local_runtime.BridgeBackedTaskTests.test_start_services_blocks_dependents_after_dependency_failure tests.test_text_renderers.LocalRuntimeErrorRendererTests.test_service_actions_task_rows_prefer_status_when_result_absent; python3 -m unittest tests.test_runtime_manager tests.test_text_renderers; python3 -m unittest tests.test_local_runtime; python3 -m unittest tests.test_runtime_hotspot_units tests.test_text_renderers; git diff --check. title: Pressure-aware Skillbox build offload and storage guard
description:
End vision: Skillbox becomes pressure-aware for agent-heavy coding work. Local agents get explicit disk-pressure posture, large build/test commands can route to non-production remote workers through remote_compilation_helper, and storage_ballast_helper protects the operator machine and selected Skillbox boxes from disk-full incidents without touching protected agent history or production Sweet Potato infrastructure.

Default target from current evidence is the managed non-production Tailnet box `portfolio-devbox` (`skillbox-portfolio-devbox`, 100.86.253.9), not `jeremy`, not `ssh-info`, and not `sweet-potato-prod`. All destructive cleanup/install/deploy work must remain blocked until the target and policy are reverified in a child issue.

Epic is complete only when:
- Skillbox has a read-only pressure/build-offload report that can name disk pressure, protected paths, cleanup candidates, RCH posture, and SBH posture.
- RCH and SBH are optional managed artifacts/services/checks in the runtime graph, with safe disabled-by-default behavior.
- Agent context/focus/stewardship surfaces tell agents when to avoid local builds and how to route expensive build/test commands.
- `portfolio-devbox` is validated as the non-production Tailnet target before any remote install/deploy/probe.
- `jeremy`, `ssh-info`, and `sweet-potato-prod` are explicitly excluded from write/deploy actions.
- Tests/docs are updated and repo-native validation passes or concrete blockers are recorded.

title: Validate box_id in box.py CLI argparse (parity with MCP _validate_identifier)
description: scripts/box.py accepts box_id as a free-form argparse string with no type= validator (cmd_up/cmd_down/etc; contrast --host/--ssh-user which use _validate_host/_validate_ssh_user). The MCP server validates box_id via _validate_identifier, but box.py is a documented first-class entrypoint and the guard/install paths call it directly. Not a command-injection bug (shlex.join neutralizes argv), but: (a) the CLI is a less-guarded trust boundary than the MCP tool that wraps it; (b) the destructive-op guard builds the dry-run marker path from the raw box_id while the MCP builds it via a validated slug, so a box_id accepted by one and rejected by the other can never line up the gate marker. Fix: add type=_validate_box_id (slug validator mirroring _IDENTIFIER_RE) to every box_id argparse argument in box.py build_parser. Existing box ids (jeremy, spaps-website, portfolio-devbox) are already valid slugs.

title: Add distribution extraction + http_security security tests
description: The most trust-sensitive code in the distribution channel has zero adversarial test coverage (2026-05-23 audit). Gaps: (1) tests/distribution/test_bundle.py has NO zip-slip/symlink/absolute-path/'..' extraction tests, even though tar extraction (bundle.py ~269-277) is the load-bearing guard via filter='data' plus a manual pre-scan that only splits on '/' (misses backslash separators); (2) there is NO test file for distribution/http_security.py at all — no scheme-rejection, no file:// rejection, no redirect-rejection tests, despite it being the security-critical transport. Also worth pinning requires-python >=3.12 since filter='data' is the real extraction guard. Add the missing tests; consider replacing the redundant manual traversal check with sole reliance on filter='data' or hardening it with PurePosixPath/normpath.
